mechanism_narrative: A declining or stable federal funds rate trend, reflecting easing or accommodative monetary policy, lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month, independent of any threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is falling versus rising (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending consumption weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: When triggered, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as forward earnings estimates are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows no statistically significant outperformance (p > 0.05) versus periods when only one series accelerates, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects supply-side capacity utilization gains that support corporate margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but this relationship weakens or reverses once the cycle turns due to overheating risk.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising policy rates
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods identified by elevated FEDFUNDS, INDPRO growth is not associated with positive forward returns, or the sign flips to negative with statistical significance (p < 0.05), invalidating the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, monetary policy is restrictive enough to tighten financial conditions materially, increasing near-term equity market volatility and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week as liquidity stress builds.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on FEDFUNDS > 5% are not significantly negative relative to unconditional average (p > 0.05) across at least two independent tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals ongoing labor market strength and consumer income support, producing a short-term positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week as risk sentiment improves.
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth sign and subsequent 5-day SPX return sign falls below 52% hit rate over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no persistent short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts of more than 10% from a rolling 6-month peak signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and foreshadows a broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from trailing 6-month peak
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rate-sensitive housing slowdown
failure_condition: Triggered instances show 60-day forward returns that are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or are positive in more than 55% of cases, rejecting the housing-led slowdown mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: During a stagflation-like regime where CPI inflation remains elevated while unemployment simultaneously rises, the combination signals a policy dilemma that increases uncertainty about future rate paths, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, whereas either signal alone in a normal regime has little predictive power.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3% AND UNRATE rising over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods do not produce forward returns significantly more negative than periods with only elevated CPI or only rising UNRATE (p > 0.05), indicating the interaction adds no incremental signal beyond the additive effect of each series.
